DISCOVERY 612, NTC OF MTN AND MTN TO COMPEL RESPONSES BY PLTF'S TO 1) FORM INTERROGATORIES; AND 2) REQ. FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS - HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES BY PLAINTIFF TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. LATE OPPOSITION HAS NOT BEEN CONSIDERED. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO RESPOND WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $500, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reclassify Action From Limited To Unlimited Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jul-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Warren Metlitzky, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, 554-3916 for defendant and opposing party City and County of San Francisco

RULING:
JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHERINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DID NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE.
THE COURT PASSED THE MATTER TO GIVE THE PLAINTIFF AN OPPORTUNITY TO APPEAR. THE MATTER WAS RE-CALLED AT 10AM. PLAINTIFF HAD STILL NOT APPEARDED EVEN THOUGH THERE WAS A HEARING REQUIRED. NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF JANET MCISAAC'S Notice Of Motion To Reclassify Action From Limited To Unlimited Jurisdiction. NO OPPOSITION FILED. Moving party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Candace Yount, CSR # 2737 (301/PJB)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses By Pltf'S To 1) Form Interrogatories; And 2) Req. For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Sep-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Warren Metlitzky, Atty. for defendant and moving party: City and County of San Francisco
George Joseph Engler, Atty. for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Janet McIsaac

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED as modified: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Motion To Compel Responses By Plaintiff To Form Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents is granted. Late opposition has not been considered. Plaintiff's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $500, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
